validation_commands: make -C src/core clean && make -C src/core (clean core build, no warnings)
grep for wx-config/WX_CXXFLAGS in src/core/Makefile (none present)
validation_outcome: Core module builds clean with no warnings and stays wx-free (src/core/Makefile has zero wx-config/WX_CXXFLAGS references). Top-level make completed with exit code 0: all module static libraries, both executables, and all test runners built and linked. No behavioral tests were added by the implementer; behavioral coverage (embedded-NUL round-trip, 64 KB readString cap no-allocation rejection, little-endian byte layout, error-code return on failed stream) is delegated to the Tester per the handoff.
acceptance_criteria_status: readString returns nonzero and allocates nothing on >64 KB length or failed stream; embedded-NUL round-trip preserved via length-prefixed os.write(s.data(), s.size()) + s.assign: SATISFIED
writeString/readString and writeU32/readU32 round-trip counts/lengths losslessly in explicit little-endian byte order: SATISFIED
write/read/writeString/readString return nonzero on failed stream and 0 on success: SATISFIED
64 KB cap and magic/version are named constants (kMaxSerializedStringBytes, kSaveMagic, kSaveFormatVersion) not inline literals: SATISFIED
core module remains wx-free and builds clean: SATISFIED
security_notes: Untrusted-input hardening: readString validates decoded length against the 64 KB cap BEFORE allocating (unbounded-allocation DoS guard); all read/write helpers propagate stream failure via nonzero return (truncated/garbage-stream abort hook for downstream loaders); length-prefixed os.write replaces the c_str() call that truncated payloads at the first embedded NUL. No secrets, disabled checks, broadened permissions, or unsafe defaults introduced.
